mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides a framework for integrating storage services into Kubernetes, making it ideal for applications requiring scalable and resilient storage solutions.

steps.title: Install Rook Ceph Operator
steps.description:
1. Clone the Rook repository:
```bash
git clone --single-branch --branch v1.16.0 https://github.com/rook/rook.git
cd rook/deploy/examples
```
2. Create the Rook operator namespace:
```bash
kubectl create namespace rook-ceph
```
3. Apply the Rook CRDs:
```bash
kubectl apply -f crds.yaml
```
4. Deploy the Rook operator:
```bash
kubectl apply -f operator.yaml -n rook-ceph
```
5. Verify the operator is running:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph get pods
```

steps.title: Create a Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
1. Apply the Ceph cluster configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f cluster.yaml -n rook-ceph
```
2. Verify the Ceph cluster status:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephclusters.ceph.rook.io
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
1. Enable monitoring for the Ceph cluster:
```bash
kubectl apply -f toolbox.yaml -n rook-ceph
```
2. Set up RBAC for accessing Ceph:
```bash
kubectl apply -f rbac.yaml -n rook-ceph
```

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check the overall status of the Rook and Ceph components:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph get pods
kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephclusters.ceph.rook.io
kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephblockpools.ceph.rook.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
1. Delete the Ceph cluster:
```bash
kubectl delete cephcluster <cluster-name> -n rook-ceph
```
2. Remove the Rook operator:
```bash
kubectl delete -f operator.yaml -n rook-ceph
```
3. Delete the CRDs:
```bash
kubectl delete -f crds.yaml
```
4. Delete the Rook namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace rook-ceph
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
1. Backup your Ceph cluster data:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it <toolbox-pod> -- ceph osd pool export <pool-name> > backup.pool
```
2. Update the Rook operator version in the operator.yaml file to the desired version (e.g., v1.16.0).
3. Apply the updated operator:
```bash
kubectl apply -f operator.yaml -n rook-ceph
```
4. Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph get pods
kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephclusters.ceph.rook.io
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Rook operator pod is not starting, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph logs <operator-pod>
```
Common issues include missing CRDs or incorrect RBAC permissions. Ensure all necessary resources are applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph Cluster Not Healthy
troubleshooting.description:
To check the health of the Ceph cluster, run:
```bash
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it <toolbox-pod> -- ceph health
```
If the cluster is unhealthy, check for OSDs that are down or misconfigured pools.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources for Ceph OSDs
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter issues with OSDs not starting due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests in your cluster.yaml file. Adjust the requests and limits as necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Improved stability and performance enhancements for Ceph integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy configuration options in cluster.yaml.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new storage classes and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Rook Ceph Operator includes a running operator, a healthy Ceph cluster, and properly configured storage classes. Verify that all components are operational and accessible.
codeSnippets:
git clone --single-branch --branch v1.16.0 https://github.com/rook/rook.git
cd rook/deploy/examples

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
